title: Plan the invalidation strategy
description: Map every write path to an invalidation mechanism so reads reflect writes within a bound.
body: Steps: 1. Enumerate every write path that mutates cached data. 2. For each, choose invalidate-on-write (explicit delete), TTL expiry, or versioned-key swap. 3. Map which keys each write touches, including derived and aggregate keys. 4. Use explicit invalidation for low-staleness-tolerance data and TTL for the rest. 5. Add a backstop TTL even on explicitly-invalidated keys to bound drift from missed events. Rollback point: before switching a key to explicit-only invalidation. Validation: write then read returns the new value within the documented bound.
